title: EDR (Endpoint Detection and Response)

headings.text: Scenario: phishing Word doc → macro → PowerShell → obfuscated download → payload injected into svchost.exe → attacker gains access.

overview: Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) instruments endpoints (workstations, servers, VMs, cloud workloads, IoT) with a sensor/agent that collects granular telemetry and forwards it to a central console where correlation, ML and rules generate alerts. The EDR also provides response controls (isolate, kill, quarantine, remote shell, artifact collection). EDR evol

study.overview: Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) instruments endpoints (workstations, servers, VMs, cloud workloads, IoT) with a sensor/agent that collects granular telemetry and forwards it to a central console where correlation, ML and rules generate alerts. The EDR also provides response controls (isolate, kill, quarantine, remote shell, artifact collection). EDR evol
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Scenario: phishing Word doc → macro → PowerShell → obfuscated download → payload injected into svchost.exe → attacker gains access.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
